title: Pat Hopkins
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Pat Hopkins
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
biography: Assistant professor of English and Director of African-American Studies at Christopher Newport University, where she researches: violence inflicted upon the black female body and images of black motherhood in literature.
education_entries: Ph D in English Literature, University of Pennsylvania
MA in Engish Literature, University of Pennsylvania
BA in Engllish Literature and Am. Hist, Queens College, City University of New York
research_interests: Dr Hopkins' research interests include African-American Literature and Gender Studies; looking at the violence inflicted upon the Black female body in cases of sexual exploitation, rape & lynching; and the complex images of black motherhood, which she has written about and presented upon extensively
teaching_interests: Dr Hopkins teaches African-American Literature, Black Literature & Film, Black Poetics and Poetry; African-American Science Fiction; American Literature, as well as Composition; however, African-American Literature is her area of specialization.
